title: Productivity Hackathon
description: A hackathon focused on building tools to enhance productivity and efficiency.
goal: Build tools that drastically improve productivity and efficiency.
criteria:
[{"id": null, "name": "Innovation & Originality", "description": "How unique and original is the productivity tool?

**Low (1-2):** The project is essentially a "Hello World" of productivity (e.g., a basic CRUD to-do list with no unique features).
**High (4-5):** The team identified a unique bottleneck—like "context switching" or "decision fatigue"—and built a tool specifically to kill that problem.", "score": 5, "max_score": 5}, {"id": null, "name": "Theme Alignment (Impact)", "description": "Does the app actually save time or remove friction?

**Low (1-2):** The app might be cool, but it doesn't actually make the user more efficient.
**High (4-5):** Does the app actually save time? Does it remove friction? A 5/5 project makes the judges want to start using the app immediately for their own work.", "score": 5, "max_score": 5}, {"id": null, "name": "Technical Execution", "description": "Quality of implementation and technical complexity.

**Low (1-2):** Code is messy, or the "app" is just a series of static HTML pages with no logic.
**High (4-5):** The team integrated complex elements (e.g., AI APIs, browser extensions, real-time sync, or advanced data visualization) successfully within the hackathon timeframe.", "score": 5, "max_score": 5}, {"id": null, "name": "User Experience (UX/UI)", "description": "Design for focus, minimal distractions, and clear feedback.

**Low (1-2):** Buttons don't work, text is unreadable, or the workflow is frustrating.
**High (4-5):** For productivity tools, **less is more**. Points are awarded for "Flow State" design—minimal distractions, keyboard shortcuts, and clear feedback.", "score": 5, "max_score": 5}, {"id": null, "name": "Pitch & Demo", "description": "Quality of the presentation and live demonstration.

**Low (1-2):** The team spent too much time on the technical stack and forgot to show the actual product.
**High (4-5):** The team clearly demonstrated a "use case." They showed exactly how a user's life is better after using their tool.", "score": 5, "max_score": 5}]
bonus_criteria: [{"id": null, "name": "Clean Code", "description": "Repository is well-documented with a clear README.", "score": 2, "max_score": 2}, {"id": null, "name": "Accessibility", "description": "Project considers screen readers, high contrast, or keyboard-only navigation.", "score": 1, "max_score": 1}, {"id": null, "name": "Wow Factor", "description": "A specific feature that made the judges say \"Wait, how did they build that in 24 hours?\"", "score": 2, "max_score": 2}]
